refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain representation, which is a fundamental aspect of worksite area analysis and remains solution-neutral by not specifying how the analysis is performed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating key site features relevant to planning and safety
Integrate terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules
Provide an interface for updating and validating the worksite model against operational policies and safety plans
selected_dp.selected_dp: Integrate terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and feature data into a single model may create dependencies between data sources
coupling_check.notes: By selecting a unified worksite model DP, the design supports independence from specific data acquisition methods and allows planning/execution functions to access a consistent representation, minimizing coupling at the interface level.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain and site feature data
Risk that integration complexity may increase if data sources are heterogeneous or updated asynchronously
Assumes that operational policies and safety plans can be encoded or referenced within the model or its metadata
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating terrain representation and semantic feature annotation as distinct DPs if data sources or update frequencies differ significantly, to reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for integrating sensor data and environment modeling to support planning and execution

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives terrain and feature data and provides unified worksite model updates to planning and execution modules

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands related to worksite modeling and integration tasks

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution is designed to handle environment data integration; low diffusion as most data processing stays internal.
Vehicle module primarily handles vehicle control; integrating terrain data would cause significant diffusion outside this module.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; integrating terrain data would be out of scope and cause high diffusion.
Safety Services is focused on safety alerts and monitoring; integrating terrain data is unrelated and would cause very high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP as it is designed to integrate environment data and provide a unified model, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on external data sources may require careful interface management.
If NGHE Solution grows too large, it may become a bottleneck or single point of failure.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly addresses the FR by specifying integration of terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules, directly fulfilling the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is appropriately chosen as the IM boundary with high cohesion (containment score 8) and low diffusion (diffusion score 2). Other modules show high diffusion scores if tasked with this DP, supporting the selection. No evidence of DP diffusion across multiple IMs is present, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the unified worksite model generated by the NGHE Solution can be validated against acceptance criteria requiring availability of the model to planning and execution functions for agreed site features.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear since the NGHE Solution module owns the DP implementation, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry and Tasking) supporting data flow and command reception.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear evidence that the DP addresses the FR, the IM boundary is well chosen with low diffusion risk, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are established. There are no recommended revisions and no diffusion risks or verification issues remain. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to stop decomposition here to maintain design clarity and avoid unnecessary complexity.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can reliably integrate terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model.
Interfaces (Telemetry and Tasking) are sufficient for data flow and command reception to support the DP implementation.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the unified worksite model meets acceptance criteria for planning and execution functions.
